factors.summary: The company is repurchasing shares, signaling confidence in its future growth.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by financing activities during the six months ended June 30, 2024, increased $366.6 million compared to the same period in 2023, primarily due to the proceeds from the issuance of the 5.600% Senior Notes. The increase was partially offset by an increase in the cash used to repurchase shares compared to the same period in 2023.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, supported by strong operating cash flows and financial flexibility.

evidence.text: Our business operates with strong operating cash flows, low leverage and low capital intensity, providing financial flexibility, including sufficient access to credit markets. Based upon our operations, existing cash balances and unused borrowing capacity under the Revolving Facility, as of June 30, 2024, we expect our cash flows from operations will be sufficient to maintain a sound financial position and liquidity and to meet our current financing needs for at least the next 12 months.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, reflecting confidence in future growth opportunities.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities during the six months ended June 30, 2024, increased $104.8 million compared to the same period in 2023, primarily due to cash used for acquisitions and higher capital expenditures.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are not exposing the company to significant risks due to fixed-rate debt.

evidence.text: Of our total outstanding indebtedness as of June 30, 2024, approximately 91% incurs fixed-rate interest and is therefore not exposed to the risk of rising variable interest rates.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant impact on margins.

evidence.text: The Term Facility requires quarterly principal payments through its maturity on November 18, 2026. Future payments total $6.3 million for the remainder of 2024, $21.9 million due in 2025, and $190.6 million due in 2026. We repaid $6.3 million of principal on the Term Facility during the six months ended June 30, 2024.

factors.summary: Overall demand is stable with mid-single digits revenue growth.

evidence.text: In the second quarter of 2024, we experienced a mid-single digits percent increase in revenue as compared to the same period in 2023, driven by favorable pricing and volume growth from most of our major businesses. We expect the business to continue to grow in the second half of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Business and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is positive, with mid-single digits growth in the second quarter and expectations for continued growth.

evidence.text: In the second quarter of 2024, we experienced a mid-single digits percent increase in revenue as compared to the same period in 2023, driven by favorable pricing and volume growth from most of our major businesses. We expect the business to continue to grow in the second half of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Business and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: Volumes are growing, contributing to revenue increases in both the three and six-month periods.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased by 5.8%, or $53.1 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 2.7% Volume 2.5% Acquisitions 0.9% Currency exchange rates (0.3)% Total 5.8% The increase in Net revenues was driven by higher volumes, improved pricing across our major businesses, and the acquisitions made during the year, which was partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with pricing contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased by 5.8%, or $53.1 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 2.7% Volume 2.5% Acquisitions 0.9% Currency exchange rates (0.3)% Total 5.8% The increase in Net revenues was driven by higher volumes, improved pricing across our major businesses, and the acquisitions made during the year, which was partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: The company is able to offset input costs through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: The increase in Operating income and Operating margin was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix and the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity. These increases were partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements and higher acquisition, integration and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind with growth expectations in both segments.

evidence.text: We expect the business to continue to grow in the second half of 2024. Net revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased by 5.8%, or $53.1 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 2.7% Volume 2.5% Acquisitions 0.9% Currency exchange rates (0.3)% Total 5.8% The increase in Net revenues was driven by higher volumes, improved pricing across our major businesses, and the acquisitions made during the year, which was partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: The factor 'forward_guidance' presents mixed signals. While the 'Other Information' subsection indicates no explicit forward-looking statements, the 'Business and Industry Trends and Outlook' subsection suggests management anticipates continued growth in the second half of 2024. This creates a situation where there is no direct forward guidance from the first source, but a clear expectation of growth from the second source. Therefore, the overall guidance is mixed, as one part provides no explicit forward-looking statements, while the other offers a positive outlook on future growth.

evidence.text: The text does not contain any explicit forward-looking statements regarding future revenue, earnings, or margins.
We expect the business to continue to grow in the second half of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Business and Industry Trends and Outlook

factors.summary: Margins are stable and showing signs of expansion. The strong operating cash flows and financial flexibility support this stability, while pricing and productivity improvements contribute to the expansion of gross and operating margins.

evidence.text: Our business operates with strong operating cash flows, low leverage and low capital intensity, providing financial flexibility, including sufficient access to credit markets.

Operating margin, which we define as Operating income as a percentage of total Net revenues, for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased to 21.6% from 20.2% for the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing and productivity in excess of inflation and investment spending 12.8% Volume / product mix 13.0% Acquisitions 1.4% Currency exchange rates (1.2)% Acquisition / integration/ restructuring expenses (1.6)% The increase in Operating income and Operating margin was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix and the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity.

factors.summary: The company is actively engaging in acquisition activities, which are contributing positively to both revenue and cash flow. These acquisitions are part of strategic growth initiatives and are supported by increased net revenues and investing activities.

evidence.text: Long-term financing needs depend largely on potential growth opportunities, including potential acquisitions, repayment or refinancing of our long-term obligations and repurchases of our ordinary shares.

Net cash used in investing activities during the six months ended June 30, 2024, increased $104.8 million compared to the same period in 2023, primarily due to cash used for acquisitions and higher capital expenditures.

Net revenues for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased by 5.8%, or $53.1 million, compared with the same period in 2023, due to the following: Pricing 2.7% Volume 2.5% Acquisitions 0.9% Currency exchange rates (0.3)% Total 5.8%

The increase in Net revenues was driven by higher volumes, improved pricing across our major businesses, and the acquisitions made during the year, which was partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

factors.summary: The company is effectively managing costs through restructuring initiatives and efficiency programs, which are contributing to margin improvements. However, these efforts are partially offset by higher restructuring and integration expenses.

evidence.text: Our short-term financing needs primarily consist of working capital requirements, restructuring initiatives, capital spending, dividend payments and principal and interest payments on our long-term debt.

The increase in Operating income and Operating margin was driven by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and investment spending, favorable volume/product mix and the contribution to operating income from recent acquisition activity. These increases were partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements and higher acquisition, integration and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: The company has improved its financial position through deleveraging and refinancing, which has strengthened its balance sheet and reduced interest rate exposure. The increased borrowing capacity under the amended Credit Facilities reflects confidence in managing debt, with extended maturity and transition to a more stable benchmark rate.

evidence.text: Of our total outstanding indebtedness as of June 30, 2024, approximately 91% incurs fixed-rate interest and is therefore not exposed to the risk of rising variable interest rates. Based upon our operations, existing cash balances and unused borrowing capacity under the Revolving Facility, as of June 30, 2024, we expect our cash flows from operations will be sufficient to maintain a sound financial position and liquidity and to meet our current financing needs for at least the next 12 months.

On May 20, 2024, the Company amended and restated the Credit Facilities which, among other things, (i) increased the total commitment on the Revolving Facility from $500.0 million to $750.0 million, (ii) extended the maturity of the Revolving Facility from November 18, 2026 to May 20, 2029, (iii) transitioned the benchmark interest rate from the Bloomberg Short-Term Bank Yield Index (''BSBY'') to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (''SOFR'') for the Credit Facilities, and (iv) added Allegion (Ireland) Finance Designated Activity Company (''Allegion Ireland DAC''), the Company’s wholly-owned subsidiary, as borrower.
